eras.name: Medieval Alpine Pragmatism
eras.description: A focus on heavy, durable woolens and linens suited for high-altitude pastoral life. Clothing was strictly functional, emphasizing protection against the elements with minimal ornamentation.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 14th-century Swiss peasant attire, thick dark brown loden wool tunic, heavy woolen leggings, a hood covering the shoulders, rugged leather boots, set against a backdrop of a rustic stone alpine hut.

eras.name: The Reformation & Sumptuary Laws
eras.description: Under the influence of Calvin and Zwingli, Swiss fashion became notoriously austere. Dark colors, particularly black, dominated the urban centers of Geneva and Zurich to signal piety and discipline.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 16th-century Geneva merchant in a high-quality black wool doublet, a stiff white pleated ruff collar, modest black hat, minimalist and somber aesthetic, oil painting style.

eras.name: The Golden Age of St. Gallen Lace
eras.description: Switzerland became a global leader in textile machinery and exquisite embroidery. This era saw the rise of intricate lace used in Parisian haute couture, reflecting immense industrial wealth.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Late 19th-century Victorian gown featuring intricate St. Gallen mechanical embroidery, heavy white lace detailing on sleeves and neckline, silk taffeta skirt, elegant studio portrait.

eras.name: Belle Époque Alpine Chic
eras.description: The rise of winter tourism in St. Moritz introduced functional luxury. Traditional Tracht elements were refined for international elites visiting for skiing and health retreats.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Early 20th-century skiers in St. Moritz, wearing heavy tweed Norfolk jackets, wool knickerbockers, thick patterned knit sweaters, and leather lace-up ski boots.

eras.name: Post-War Precision Modernism
eras.description: Swiss fashion aligned with the 'Swiss Style' of graphic design—clean lines, high-quality fabrics, and a focus on longevity. This period solidified the reputation for high-end ready-to-wear.
eras.aiImagePrompt: 1960s Swiss woman in a perfectly tailored navy blue wool suit, minimalist lines, modernist aesthetic, standing in front of a mid-century modern concrete building in Zurich.

eras.name: Contemporary Technical Luxury
eras.description: Modern Swiss fashion merges high-tech textile innovation with understated luxury. There is a strong emphasis on performance wear and sustainable, circular design principles.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Modern Zurich street style, high-tech waterproof minimalist parka, sleek tapered trousers, high-end sneakers, carrying a Freitag-style messenger bag, urban alpine background.

textiles.name: St. Gallen Lace
textiles.description: World-renowned mechanical embroidery and lace that has supplied brands like Chanel and Dior for decades.

textiles.name: Swiss Dot
textiles.description: A lightweight cotton fabric characterized by small, raised tufts or 'dots' woven into the surface.

textiles.name: Etamine
textiles.description: A fine, gauze-like fabric often used for traditional veils and high-end curtains or summer clothing.

climate.fashionImplication: The variable climate necessitates high-performance layering and durable outerwear, leading to a national preference for 'Quiet Luxury' that prioritizes material quality over flashiness.
